jobs.name: hf-new-skill-every-5m
jobs.prompt:
Hugging Face learning loop. Task: every time this cron fires, do ONE focused deep-dive into a NEW, specific topic related to the Hugging Face / AI ecosystem. Do NOT repeat any topic you have already explored before. After researching, create a NEW, unique Hermes skill that captures what you learned. The skill must be a self-contained markdown file ready to be loaded. Save it to: ~/.hermes/profiles/hermesagent/skills/mlops/hf-<slug>/SKILL.md (replace spaces/hyphens in the slug). Then save a durable entry to Supermemory summarizing the new skill topic, file path, and key facts.

Operational constraints:
- Scope is informational/educational only. No local training, no environment mutations, no uploads, no tokens, no GPU work.
- Use web_search and/or web_extract for research.
- Always check ~/.hermes/profiles/hermesagent/skills/ first, so you don't create a skill you already have.
- Also search Supermemory with query=\"hf skill\" before choosing a topic.
- If your research suggests nothing genuinely new or useful, stay quiet: save a Supermemory entry saying no-op this tick, then output \"\" so nothing is sent to the user.
- If you are not sure about something, say so instead of fabricating.
- Do not use the same topic twice. Choose a subtopic you have not covered.

Required output at end of the loop (this text is auto-delivered to Beer):
- Brief confirmation line: \"Learned <TOPIC>. New skill saved to <PATH>.\"
- Bullet list of 3-5 key facts you learned.
- Optional:"""

jobs.name: supermemory-sync
jobs.prompt: Review our recent conversation/session memory and persist anything important and durable to long-term Supermemory using the supermemory memory tool. Reply in one line: what you saved, or "nothing new".
